Items.Title: GitHub Actions 简化 DevOps 工作流程的力量 | The Power of GitHub Actions for Streamlining DevOps Workflows
Items.Description:
凭借与各种工具和服务集成的能力，GitHub Actions 允许开发人员简化他们的工作流程并专注于交付高质量的代码。

在本文中，我们将深入探讨 GitHub Actions 的基本要素，并了解它们如何提高 DevOps 流程的效率。 除此之外，我将重点介绍目前可用的一些很棒的 GitHub Actions，并分享一些开发人员如何利用 GitHub Actions 来增强其工作流程的真实示例。

那么，GitHub Action 到底是什么？

它于 2018 年推出，从那时起它已成为DevOps 生态系统的重要组成部分。 它使开发人员能够为他们的项目设计个性化的工作流程，并协助实现传统上由手工执行的任务的自动化。 这可以节省时间并消除开发人员手动干预的需要，从而使他们能够专注于更重要的职责。

这意味着开发人员可以使用 GitHub Actions 来自动化跨越多个工具和服务的任务，从而创建一个无缝且高效的工作流程。

开发人员可以使用 GitHub Actions 自动执行Python 、 Java和JavaScript等语言的任务，使其成为适用于任何开发团队的多功能工具。

它允许开发人员自动执行重复或耗时的任务。 这有助于减少花在这些任务上的时间，让开发人员腾出时间专注于更重要的工作。

  • 它可以帮助改善开发团队之间的协作。 它允许开发人员在整个团队中共享工作流和自动化任务，使开发人员更容易在项目上一起工作和协作。

  • 它还可以帮助提高代码质量。 通过自动化测试和构建代码等任务，GitHub Actions 可以帮助在开发过程中及早发现缺陷和错误，从而产生更高质量和高度可读的代码。

    • 许多组织正在使用 GitHub Actions 来简化他们的 DevOps 工作流程。 以下是开发人员如何在其工作流程中使用 GitHub Actions 的一些示例：

    软件开发操作💡：软件开发团队使用 GitHub Actions 来自动化他们的测试和部署过程。 通过将 GitHub Actions 与其测试和部署工具集成，他们能够自动化从代码提交到部署的整个过程。 这可以帮助整个团队和公司减少部署时间并提高代码质量。

    通过使用 GitHub Actions，他们能够在部署代码之前对其代码运行自动化测试，确保他们的代码没有缺陷和错误。 这可以帮助团队提高代码质量，还可以帮助他们减少部署时间。

    通过将 GitHub Actions 与他们的构建工具集成，他们能够自动化构建移动应用程序的过程，从而更轻松地创建和发布新版本的应用程序。 这可以帮助团队节省时间并专注于创建高质量的代码。

    用于简化 DevOps 工作流程的最佳 GitHub Actions

    现在有许多 GitHub Actions 可以帮助简化 DevOps 工作流程。 以下是当今可用的一些最佳 GitHub Actions：

    通过此操作，开发人员可以轻松地将 AWS 服务集成到他们的工作流程中，例如EC2 、 S3和Lambda 。 这可以通过在 AWS 平台上自动执行部署、测试和扩展等任务来帮助简化 DevOps 工作流程。

    通过此操作，开发人员可以轻松地将 Azure 服务集成到他们的工作流中，例如Azure Functions 、 App Services和Kubernetes 。 这可以通过在 Azure 平台上自动执行部署、测试和缩放等任务来帮助简化 DevOps 工作流。

    通过此操作，开发人员可以轻松地将 Terraform 集成到他们的工作流中，并自动执行诸如供应、更新和部署基础设施等任务。 这可以通过自动化基础架构管理并确保它始终保持最新和合规来帮助简化 DevOps 工作流程。

    通过此操作，开发人员可以轻松地将 Docker 集成到他们的工作流中，并自动执行构建、推送和运行Docker 镜像和容器等任务。 这可以通过自动管理 Docker 容器并确保它们始终是最新的并准备好部署来帮助简化 DevOps 工作流程。

    结论

    它能够与各种工具和服务进行交互，并支持多种编程语言，这使其成为任何开发团队的多功能和宝贵资源。 通过自动化测试、开发和部署代码等操作，GitHub Actions 使开发人员能够专注于生成高质量代码并增强开发团队内部的合作。 借助当今可用的最佳可访问 GitHub 操作，开发人员可以简单地简化他们的 DevOps 工作流程并提高他们的工作流程效率。



    原文:

    GitHub Actions is a powerful tool for automating development workflows, and it has quickly become a staple in the DevOps world. With its ability to integrate with various tools and services, GitHub Actions allows developers to streamline their workflow and focus on delivering high-quality code.

    In this article, we'll dive into the essentials of GitHub Actions and see how they might improve the efficiency of your DevOps processes. In addition to that, I'll highlight some of the awesome GitHub Actions currently available and share some real-world examples of how developers are utilizing GitHub Actions to enhance their workflow.

    So, what exactly is GitHub Action?

    GitHub Actions is a feature/tool of GitHub for automating software development workflows. It was introduced in 2018, and since then it it has become an essential part of the DevOps ecosystem. GitHub Actions allows developers to automate tasks such as testing, building, and deploying code, making it easier to focus on creating high-quality code. It provide developers with the ability to design individualized workflows for their projects and assist in the automation of tasks that are traditionally carried out by hand. This can save time and eliminate the need for developers to manually intervene, which frees them up to concentrate on more critical duties.

    One of the key features of GitHub Actions is its ability to integrate with various tools and services. This means that developers can use GitHub Actions to automate tasks that span across multiple tools and services, creating a seamless and efficient workflow.

    Another key feature of GitHub Actions is its support for various programming languages. Developers can use GitHub Actions to automate tasks in languages such as Python, Java, and JavaScript, making it a versatile tool for any development teams.

    How GitHub Actions Can Streamline DevOps Workflows?

    GitHub Actions can help streamline DevOps workflows in several ways:

    • It allows developers to automate tasks that are repetitive or time-consuming. This can help reduce the time spent on these tasks, freeing up developers to focus on more important work.

    • It can help improve collaboration among development teams. It allows developers to share workflows and automate tasks across the team, making it easier for developers to work together and collaborate on projects.

    • It can also help improve the quality of code. By automating tasks such as testing and building code, GitHub Actions can help catch bugs and errors earlier in the development process, leading to higher-quality and highly readable code..

    Real-World Examples of GitHub Actions in DevOps Workflows

    GitHub Actions is being used by many organizations to streamline their DevOps workflows. Here are some examples of how developers are using GitHub Actions in their workflow:

    Actions for software development💡: A software development team uses GitHub Actions to automate their testing and deployment process. By integrating GitHub Actions with their testing and deployment tools, they are able to automate the entire process, from code submission to deployment. This can help the entire team as well as company reduce their deployment time and improve the quality of their code.

    Actions for web development💻: A web development team uses GitHub Actions to automate their testing and deployment process. By using GitHub Actions, they are able to run automated tests on their code before it is deployed, ensuring that their code is free of bugs and errors. This can help the team improve the quality of their code, and it can also help them reduce their deployment time.

    Actions for mobile development📱: A mobile development team uses GitHub Actions to automate their build process. By integrating GitHub Actions with their build tools, they are able to automate the process of building their mobile app, making it easier to create and release new versions of their app. This can help the team save time and focus on creating high-quality code.

    The Best GitHub Actions for Streamlining DevOps Workflows

    There are many GitHub Actions available today that can help streamline DevOps workflows. Here are some of the best GitHub Actions available today:

    GitHub Actions for AWS: This GitHub Action allows developers to automate tasks on the Amazon Web Services (AWS) platform. With this action, developers can easily integrate AWS services into their workflows, such as EC2, S3, and Lambda. This can help streamline DevOps workflows by automating tasks such as deployment, testing, and scaling on the AWS platform.

    GitHub Actions for Azure: This GitHub Action allows developers to automate tasks on the Microsoft Azure platform. With this action, developers can easily integrate Azure services into their workflows, such as Azure Functions, App Services, and Kubernetes. This can help streamline DevOps workflows by automating tasks such as deployment, testing, and scaling on the Azure platform.

    GitHub Actions for Terraform: This GitHub Action allows developers to automate tasks using the Terraform infrastructure as code tool. With this action, developers can easily integrate Terraform into their workflows, and automate tasks such as provisioning, updating, and deploying the infrastructure. This can help streamline DevOps workflows by automating the management of infrastructure, and ensuring that it is always up-to-date and compliant.

    GitHub Actions for Docker: This GitHub Action allows developers to automate tasks using the Docker container platform. With this action, developers can easily integrate Docker into their workflows, and automate tasks such as building, pushing, and running Docker images and containers. This can help streamline DevOps workflows by automating the management of Docker containers, and ensuring that they are always up-to-date and ready to deploy.

    Conclusion

    GitHub Actions is a really powerfulll and effective tool for streamlining DevOps processes. Its ability to interface with a variety of tools and services, as well as its support for a number of programming languages, make it a versatile and valuable resource for any development team. By automating operations like as testing, developing, and deploying code, GitHub Actions enables developers to focus on producing high-quality code and enhances cooperation within development teams. With the best accessible GitHub actions available today, developers can simply streamline their DevOps workflows and boost their workflow efficiency.

Items.Title: 使用 Bash 使你的 Tmux 状态行 100% 更好 | Make Your Tmux Status Line 100% Better With Bash
Items.Description:
您知道您可以轻松地使用 Bash 在 Tmux 状态行上显示信息吗？ 正因为如此，你能做的事情的可能性几乎是无穷无尽的。 您可以显示各种有用的信息，例如：

  • 主机名
  • 网络掩码
  • 网关
  • 记忆
  • 处理器温度
  • 系统负载
  • 电池表
  • 时间
  • 日期
  • 天气预报
  • 在本文中，我将向您展示一种向 Tmux 状态行添加信息的简单方法。

    要求

    这是默认状态行。

    这是经过增强以显示更多信息的状态行。


     # Debian/Ubuntu based distributions $ sudo apt install tmux # Redhat/Fedora based distributions $ sudo dnf install tmux

    检查你的 Tmux 版本。 目前我使用的是 3.3a 版。

     $ tmux -V # Output tmux 3.3a

    如果您没有.tmux.conf请在您的主目录中创建一个。

    我将从基本的.tmux.conf配置开始。 编辑.tmux.conf并添加以下行。

     # .tmux.conf bind r source-file ~/.tmux.conf \; display-message "RELOADING CONFIGURATION FILE…" # STATUS LINE set -g status on set -g status-interval 1 set -g status-justify centre # Careful! It is spelled "centre" not "center". set -g status-style fg = white,bg = black # Highlight the current window. setw -g window-status-current-style fg = white,bg = red,bright # LEFT STATUS set -g status-left-length 100 set -g status-left-style default set -g status-left " " # RIGHT STATUS set -g status-right-length 100 set -g status-right-style default set -g status-right " "

    状态行左侧

    查看手册页以获取更多信息。

    在双引号之间添加#h

    您需要重新加载 Tmux 才能看到更改。 要重新加载 Tmux，您可以键入exit然后再次启动 Tmux，或者您可以通过按CTRL+b然后按r来重新加载 Tmux。
    重新加载 Tmux 后，您将在状态行上看到您计算机的主机名。

    将 IP 地址和网络掩码添加到状态行

    自定义状态行所需要做的就是添加 Bash 单行代码。 要将 Bash 命令添加到命令行，您只需将每个命令放在井号和括号内#( <my-command> ) 。

    我的以太网接口是enp4s0 。 所以我使用这个 Bash 命令来获取enp4s0以太网接口的 IP 地址。

     $ ip addr show enp4s0 | awk '/inet /{printf $2}' # Output 192.168.0.101/26

    我可以通过将命令放在括号之间来将命令添加到 Tmux 状态行（enp4s0 在配置中硬编码！）。

     set -g status-left "#h #(ip addr show enp4s0 | awk '/inet /{printf $2}')"

    将 CPU 温度添加到状态行

    接下来我要添加 CPU 温度。 要完成这项工作，您必须使用lm-sensors 。 安装lm-sensors 。

     # Debian/Ubuntu based distributions $ sudo apt install lm-sensors # Redhat/Fedora based distributions $ sudo dnf install lm_sensors

    使用此命令启用 lm-sensors 并按照提示进行操作。

    运行sensors命令以查看输出。 您需要从输出中找出哪一行显示有关 CPU 的信息。 您需要的行可能标记为Tccd1或CPU 。 将-f参数添加到传感器命令以查看华氏温度。

     $ sensors -f # Partial Output intel-isa-0000 Adapter: ISA adapter fan1: 0 RPM CPU: +96.8°F GPU: N/A temp3: +96.8°F temp4: +32.0°F temp5: +96.8°F temp6: +96.8°F temp7: +96.8°F temp8: N/A intel-pci-0400 Adapter: PCI adapter vddgfx: 794.00 mV vddnb: 604.00 mV edge: +95.0°F PPT: 3.16 W BAT0-acpi-0 Adapter: ACPI interface in0: 17.27 V [ ....]

    将此代码#(sensors -f | awk '/CPU/{printf $2}')添加到左侧状态行的末尾。 此命令使用 awk 查找具有文本CPU的行并打印第二列。

     set -g status-left "#h #(ip addr show enp4s0 | awk '/inet /{printf $2 }') #(sensors -f | awk '/CPU/{printf $2 }')"

    将内存添加到状态行

    接下来，我要查看内存使用量、总内存，并计算内存使用百分比。 为此，我使用free命令。

     # Amount of memory used / total amount of memory $ free -m -h | awk '/Mem/{printf $3"/"$2}' # Output 3.3Gi/62Gi # Calculate percentage of memory used $ free -m | awk '/Mem/{printf "%.2f%", 100*$3/$2}' # Output 2.1%

    小心这里！ 要使 memory 命令在 Tmux 状态行中正确显示，需要对某些字符进行转义。
    在这里，我使用反斜杠\来转义双引号\" 、句点\. 、百分号\%和正斜杠\/ 。

    在转义字符之前：

     #(free -m -h | awk '/Mem/{printf $3"/"$2}') #(free -m | awk '/Mem{printf "%.2f%", 100*$2/$3}')"

    转义字符后：

     #(free -m -h | awk '/Mem/{printf $3\"\/\"$2}') #(free -m | awk '/Mem{printf \"\%\.2f\%\", 100*$2/$3}')"

    剩下的状态现在应该是这样的。

     set -g status-left "#h #(ip addr show enp4s0 | awk '/inet /{printf $2 }') #(sensors | awk '/CPU/{printf $2 }') #(free -m -h | awk '/Mem/{printf $3 \"\/\" $2 }') #(free -m | awk '/Mem{printf \"\%\. 2f \%\" , 100* $2 / $3 }')"

    将电池电量添加到状态行

    我想看看电池电量，看看它是否正在充电。 为此，我可以检查/sys文件系统以获取电池信息。

    如果您的终端支持unicode 字符，您可以将 unicode 字符添加到状态栏。

    我将测试电源线是否已插入，如果是则显示闪电图标。 我将在闪电旁边显示剩余电池的百分比。

    如果$(cat /sys/class/power_supply/AC/online)等于1 ，将显示闪电。

    将这些行添加到左侧状态行的末尾。

     #([ $(cat /sys/class/power_supply/AC/online) == 1 ] && printf %s'🗲') #( $(cat /sys/class/power_supply/BAT0/capacity)\% )

    左边的状态行应该看起来像这样。

     set -g status-left "#h #(ip addr show enp4s0 | awk '/inet /{printf $2 }') #(sensors | awk '/CPU/{printf $2 }') #(free -m -h | awk '/Mem/{printf \$ 3 \"\/\"\$ 2 \} ') #(free -m | awk '/Mem{printf 100* $2 / $3 " % "}') #([ $( cat /sys/class/power_supply/AC/online ) == 1 ] && printf %s'🗲') #(cat /sys/class/power_supply/BAT0/capacity) \% "

    将 VPN 添加到状态行

    我想检查 VPN 是否启动并查看分配给它的 IP 地址。 根据您使用的 VPN，您可能必须检查 tun0 或 ppp0 接口。

     ip addr show tun0 | awk '/tun0/{printf $2}' # Output 10.20.3.45

    将其添加到左侧状态行的末尾。

     #(ip addr show tun0 | awk '/tun0/{printf \"VPN: \"$2}')

    我的状态行现在看起来像这样。

     set -g status-left "#h #(ip addr show enp4s0 | awk '/inet /{printf $2 }') #(sensors | awk '/CPU/{printf $2 }') #(free -m -h | awk '/Mem/{printf \$ 3 \"\/\"\$ 2 \} ') #(free -m | awk '/Mem{printf 100* $2 / $3 " % "}') #([ $( cat /sys/class/power_supply/AC/online ) == 1 ] && printf %s'🗲') #(cat /sys/class/power_supply/BAT0/capacity) \% #(ip a | awk '/tun0/{printf \" VPN: \" $2 }')"

    状态栏右侧

    现在让我们配置右侧。

    我将使用uptime命令获取平均负载。

     $ uptime # Output 02:39:38 up 12:18, 1 user, load average: 0.20, 0.11, 0.10

    输出中的列数可能会根据时间发生变化。 为了解决这个问题，我使用 $(NF) 获取最后一列并tr删除冒号字符。

    将其添加到右侧状态行选项，双引号之间带有转义字符。

     set -g status-right "#(uptime | awk '{printf \$ (NF-2) \" \"\$ (NF-1) \" \"\$ (NF)}' | tr -d ',')"

    我要添加的最后一件事是日期、时间和时区使用变量到 status-right。 鉴于此，我可以使用自定义格式查看日期和时间。 示例： %F表示YYYY-mm-dd %T表示HH:MM:SS 。 有关更多信息，请参见手册页man strftime 。

    将其添加到右侧状态行的末尾。

     set -g status-right "#(uptime | awk '{printf \$ (NF-2) \" \"\$ (NF-1) \" \"\$ (NF)}' | tr -d ',') %F %T %Z"

    添加 Bash 脚本

    如果你需要配置比一行所能容纳的更复杂的代码，你可以制作一个单独的 Bash 文件并像这样添加它。

     set -g status-right "#( example.sh )"
     $ cat example.sh #!/bin/bash # example.sh # Get the IP address dynamically function get_ip_address () { for iface in /sys/class/net/ * /operstate ; do if [ " $( echo $iface | awk -F '/' '{print $5}' ) " != "lo" ] ; then if [ " $( cat $iface ) " == "up" ] ; then interface = $( echo $iface | awk -F '/' '{print $5}' ) printf "%s " " $( ip addr show $interface | awk '/inet /{print $2}' ) " fi fi done } get_ip_address

    添加颜色

    在您的终端中运行此代码以查看可用的颜色。

     #!/bin/bash for num in { 0..255 } ; do printf "%s \0 33[38;5; ${ num } mcolour ${ num } \0 33[0m \t " ; if [ $( expr $(( num+1 )) % 8 ) -eq 0 ] ; then printf " \n " fi done

    现在您可以为状态行添加颜色。 通过将#[fg=<colourN>]或#[bg=<colourN>]变量设置为您选择的颜色来添加前景色。
    使用#[default]返回默认颜色。

     set -g status-left "#[fg=colour220] #h #[fg=colour47] #(ip addr show enp4s0 | awk '/inet /{printf $2 }') #[colour27] #(sensors -f | awk '/CPU/{printf $2 }') #[default]"

    状态行可能会变得很长。 如果您不喜欢长行，请使用反斜杠将其分开。

     set -g status-left "#[fg=colour220]#h \ #[fg=colour196] #(ip addr show enp4s0 | awk '/inet /{printf $2 }') \ #[fg=colour39] #(sensors | awk '/CPU/{printf $2 }') \ #[fg=colour40] #(free -m -h | awk '/Mem/{printf \$ 3 \"\/\"\$ 2 \} ') \ #[fg=colour128] #(free -m | awk '/Mem{printf 100* $2 / $3 " % "}') \ #[fg=colour202] #([ $( cat /sys/class/power_supply/AC/online ) == 1 ] && printf %s'🗲') #(cat /sys/class/power_supply/BAT0/capacity) \%\ #[fg=colour7] #(ip a | awk '/tun0/{printf \" VPN: \" $2 }') \ #[default]"

    完成状态行配置

    # .tmux.conf bind r source-file ~/.tmux.conf \; display-message "RELOADING CONFIGURATION FILE…" # STATUS LINE set -g status on set -g status-interval 1 set -g status-justify centre # Careful! It is spelled "centre" not "center". set -g status-style fg = white,bg = black # Highlight the current window. setw -g window-status-current-style fg = white,bg = red,bright # LEFT STATUS set -g status-left-length 100 set -g status-left-style default set -g status-left "#[fg=colour220]#h \ #[fg=colour196] #(ip addr show enp4s0 | awk '/inet /{printf $2 }') \ #[fg=colour39] #(sensors | awk '/CPU/{printf $2 }') \ #[fg=colour40] #(free -m -h | awk '/Mem/{printf \$ 3 \"\/\"\$ 2 \} ') \ #[fg=colour128] #(free -m | awk '/Mem{printf 100* $2 / $3 " % "}') \ #[fg=colour202] #([ $( cat /sys/class/power_supply/AC/online ) == 1 ] && printf %s'🗲') #(cat /sys/class/power_supply/BAT0/capacity) \%\ #[fg=colour7] #(ip a | awk '/tun0/{printf \" VPN: \" $2 }') \ #[default]" # RIGHT STATUS set -g status-right-length 100 set -g status-right-style default set -g status-right "#[fg=colour39] #(uptime | awk '{printf \$ (NF-2) \" \"\$ (NF-1) \" \"\$ (NF)}' | tr -d ',') \ #[fg=colour40] %F \ #[fg=colour128] %T \ #[fg=colour202] %Z \ #[default]"

    您现在可以轻松地让 Bash 在 Tmux 状态行上显示有用的信息。 我希望你学到了新东西并且喜欢阅读这篇文章。

    在Dev.to和Github上关注我。

原文:

Did you know that you can easily use Bash to display information on the Tmux status line? Because of that the possibilities of what you can do are almost endless. You can display all sorts of useful information such as:

  • Hostname
  • IP address
  • Netmask
  • Gateway
  • Memory
  • CPU temperature
  • System load
  • Battery meter
  • VPN status
  • Time
  • Date
  • Weather report
  • Git status

In this article I will show you an easy way to add information to your Tmux status line.

Requirements

Here is the default status line.

Here is the status line enhanced to show more information.


# Debian/Ubuntu based distributions
$ sudo apt install tmux # Redhat/Fedora based distributions
$ sudo dnf install tmux

Check your Tmux version. Currently I am using version 3.3a.

$ tmux -V # Output
tmux 3.3a

If you do not have a .tmux.conf create one in your home directory.

I will start out with a basic .tmux.conf configuration. Edit .tmux.conf and add the following lines.


# .tmux.conf bind r source-file ~/.tmux.conf \; display-message "RELOADING CONFIGURATION FILE…" # STATUS LINE
set -g status on
set -g status-interval 1
set -g status-justify centre # Careful! It is spelled "centre" not "center".
set -g status-style fg=white,bg=black # Highlight the current window.
setw -g window-status-current-style fg=white,bg=red,bright # LEFT STATUS
set -g status-left-length 100
set -g status-left-style default
set -g status-left " " # RIGHT STATUS
set -g status-right-length 100
set -g status-right-style default
set -g status-right " "

Status Line Left Side

Tmux has same built in variables that you can use to display information such as #h to display the hostname. View the man page for more information.

Add #h in between the double quotes

You need to reload Tmux to see the changes. To reload Tmux you can type exit then launch Tmux again or you can reload Tmux by press the CTRL+b then r.
After reloading Tmux you will see the hostname of your computer on the status line.

Adding the IP address and netmask to Status Line

All we need to do to customize the status line is to add Bash one-liners. To add Bash commands to the command line you only need to place each command inside a hash mark and parenthesis #( <my-command> ).

My Ethernet interface is enp4s0. So I use this Bash command to get the IP address of the enp4s0 Ethernet interface.


$ ip addr show enp4s0 | awk '/inet /{printf $2}'

# Output

192.168.0.101/26

I can add the command to the Tmux status line by placing it in between the parenthesis like this (enp4s0 is hard coded in the configuration!).


set -g status-left "#h #(ip addr show enp4s0 | awk '/inet /{printf $2}')"

Adding the CPU Temperature to Status Line

Next I want to add the CPU temperature. To make that work you must use lm-sensors. Install lm-sensors.


# Debian/Ubuntu based distributions
$ sudo apt install lm-sensors # Redhat/Fedora based distributions
$ sudo dnf install lm_sensors

Enable lm-sensors with this command and follow the prompts.

Run the sensors command to see the output. From the ouput you will need to figure out which line displays information about the CPU. The line you need might be labeled Tccd1 or CPU. Add the -f argument to the sensors command to see the tempurature in Fahrenheit.


$ sensors -f # Partial Output  intel-isa-0000
Adapter: ISA adapter
fan1: 0 RPM
CPU: +96.8°F GPU: N/A temp3: +96.8°F temp4: +32.0°F temp5: +96.8°F temp6: +96.8°F temp7: +96.8°F temp8: N/A intel-pci-0400
Adapter: PCI adapter
vddgfx: 794.00 mV vddnb: 604.00 mV edge: +95.0°F PPT: 3.16 W BAT0-acpi-0
Adapter: ACPI interface
in0: 17.27 V [....]

Add this code #(sensors -f | awk '/CPU/{printf $2}') to the end of the left status line. This command uses awk to find the row with the text CPU and print the second column.


set -g status-left "#h #(ip addr show enp4s0 | awk '/inet /{printf $2}') #(sensors -f | awk '/CPU/{printf $2}')"

Adding Memory to the Status Line

Next, I want to view the amount of memory used, the total memory, and calculate the percentage of memory in use. To do that I use the free command.


# Amount of memory used / total amount of memory
$ free -m -h | awk '/Mem/{printf $3"/"$2}' # Output 3.3Gi/62Gi # Calculate percentage of memory used $ free -m | awk '/Mem/{printf "%.2f%", 100*$3/$2}' # Output

2.1%

Careful here! To get the memory command to display properly in the Tmux status line requires that certain characters be escaped.
Here I am using the backward slash \ to escape the double quote \", the period \., the percent sign \%, and the forward slash \/.

Before escaping characters:


#(free -m -h | awk '/Mem/{printf $3"/"$2}') #(free -m | awk '/Mem{printf "%.2f%", 100*$2/$3}')"

After escaping characters:


#(free -m -h | awk '/Mem/{printf $3\"\/\"$2}') #(free -m | awk '/Mem{printf \"\%\.2f\%\", 100*$2/$3}')"

The status left should now look like this.


set -g status-left "#h #(ip addr show enp4s0 | awk '/inet /{printf $2}') #(sensors | awk '/CPU/{printf $2}') #(free -m -h | awk '/Mem/{printf $3\"\/\"$2}') #(free -m | awk '/Mem{printf \"\%\.2f\%\", 100*$2/$3}')"

Adding the Battery Level to Status Line

I want to see the battery level and see if it is charging on not. To do that I can check the /sys file system for the battery information.

If your terminal supports unicode characters you can add an unicode characters to the status bar.

I will test to see if the power cable is plugged in and show a lighting bolt icon if it is. I will show the percentage of battery remaining next to the lightning bolt.

If $(cat /sys/class/power_supply/AC/online) is equal to 1 the lightning bolt will display.

Add these lines to the end of the left status line.


#([ $(cat /sys/class/power_supply/AC/online) == 1 ] && printf %s'🗲')  #( $(cat /sys/class/power_supply/BAT0/capacity)\% )

The left status line should look something like this.


set -g status-left "#h #(ip addr show enp4s0 | awk '/inet /{printf $2}') #(sensors | awk '/CPU/{printf $2}') #(free -m -h | awk '/Mem/{printf \$3\"\/\"\$2\}') #(free -m | awk '/Mem{printf 100*$2/$3" %"}') #([ $(cat /sys/class/power_supply/AC/online) == 1 ] && printf %s'🗲') #(cat /sys/class/power_supply/BAT0/capacity)\%"

Adding the VPN to Status Line

I want to check if the VPN is up and see the IP address assigned to it. Depending on the VPN you use you might have to check the tun0 or ppp0 interface.


ip addr show tun0 | awk '/tun0/{printf $2}' # Output

10.20.3.45

Add this to the end of the left status line.


#(ip addr show tun0 | awk '/tun0/{printf \"VPN: \"$2}')

My status line now looks like this.


set -g status-left "#h #(ip addr show enp4s0 | awk '/inet /{printf $2}') #(sensors | awk '/CPU/{printf $2}') #(free -m -h | awk '/Mem/{printf \$3\"\/\"\$2\}') #(free -m | awk '/Mem{printf 100*$2/$3" %"}') #([ $(cat /sys/class/power_supply/AC/online) == 1 ] && printf %s'🗲') #(cat /sys/class/power_supply/BAT0/capacity)\% #(ip a | awk '/tun0/{printf \"VPN: \" $2}')"

Status Bar Right Side

Now let’s configure the right side.

I will get the load average using the uptime command.


$ uptime # Output

02:39:38 up 12:18,  1 user,  load average: 0.20, 0.11, 0.10

The number of columns in the output can change so depending on the time. To get around that problem I use $(NF) to get the last column and tr to remove the colon characters.

Add this to the right status line option with escaped characters in between the double quotes.


set -g status-right "#(uptime | awk '{printf \$(NF-2)\" \"\$(NF-1)\" \"\$(NF)}' | tr -d ',')"

The last thing I will add is the date, time, and timezone using variables to status-right. Tmux uses strftime formatting to display variables. Given that, I can view the date and time using a custom format. Example: %F represents YYYY-mm-dd %T represents HH:MM:SS . See the man page for more information man strftime.

Add that to the end of the right status line.


set -g status-right "#(uptime | awk '{printf \$(NF-2)\" \"\$(NF-1)\" \"\$(NF)}' | tr -d ',') %F %T %Z"

Adding a Bash Script

If you needed to configure more complicated code than what could fit on one line you can make a separate Bash file and add it like this.


set -g status-right "#( example.sh )"



$ cat example.sh #!/bin/bash
# example.sh # Get the IP address dynamically
function get_ip_address() { for iface in /sys/class/net/*/operstate; do if [ "$(echo $iface | awk -F'/' '{print $5}')" != "lo" ]; then if [ "$(cat $iface)" == "up" ] ; then interface=$(echo $iface | awk -F'/' '{print $5}') printf "%s " "$(ip addr show $interface | awk '/inet /{print $2}')" fi fi done }

get_ip_address

Adding Color

Run this code in your terminal to see what colors you have available.


#!/bin/bash for num in {0..255}; do printf "%s\033[38;5;${num}mcolour${num}\033[0m \t"; if [ $(expr $((num+1)) % 8) -eq 0 ]; then printf "\n" fi
done

Now you can add color to your status line. Add foreground colors by setting a #[fg=<colourN>] or #[bg=<colourN>] variable to the color of your choice.
fg= is for the forground color bg= is to set the background color in between square brackets. Use #[default] to back to the default colors.


set -g status-left "#[fg=colour220] #h #[fg=colour47] #(ip addr show enp4s0 | awk '/inet /{printf $2}') #[colour27] #(sensors -f | awk '/CPU/{printf $2}') #[default]"

The status lines can get quite long. If you do not like long lines use the backslash to break it up.


set -g status-left "#[fg=colour220]#h\
#[fg=colour196] #(ip addr show enp4s0 | awk '/inet /{printf $2}')\
#[fg=colour39] #(sensors | awk '/CPU/{printf $2}')\
#[fg=colour40] #(free -m -h | awk '/Mem/{printf \$3\"\/\"\$2\}')\
#[fg=colour128] #(free -m | awk '/Mem{printf 100*$2/$3" %"}')\
#[fg=colour202] #([ $(cat /sys/class/power_supply/AC/online) == 1 ] && printf %s'🗲') #(cat /sys/class/power_supply/BAT0/capacity)\%\
#[fg=colour7] #(ip a | awk '/tun0/{printf \"VPN: \" $2}')\
#[default]"

Complete Status Line Configuration 


# .tmux.conf bind r source-file ~/.tmux.conf \; display-message "RELOADING CONFIGURATION FILE…" # STATUS LINE
set -g status on
set -g status-interval 1
set -g status-justify centre # Careful! It is spelled "centre" not "center".
set -g status-style fg=white,bg=black # Highlight the current window.
setw -g window-status-current-style fg=white,bg=red,bright # LEFT STATUS
set -g status-left-length 100
set -g status-left-style default
set -g status-left "#[fg=colour220]#h\
#[fg=colour196] #(ip addr show enp4s0 | awk '/inet /{printf $2}')\
#[fg=colour39] #(sensors | awk '/CPU/{printf $2}')\
#[fg=colour40] #(free -m -h | awk '/Mem/{printf \$3\"\/\"\$2\}')\
#[fg=colour128] #(free -m | awk '/Mem{printf 100*$2/$3" %"}')\
#[fg=colour202] #([ $(cat /sys/class/power_supply/AC/online) == 1 ] && printf %s'🗲') #(cat /sys/class/power_supply/BAT0/capacity)\%\
#[fg=colour7] #(ip a | awk '/tun0/{printf \"VPN: \" $2}')\
#[default]" # RIGHT STATUS
set -g status-right-length 100
set -g status-right-style default
set -g status-right "#[fg=colour39] #(uptime | awk '{printf \$(NF-2)\" \"\$(NF-1)\" \"\$(NF)}' | tr -d ',')\
#[fg=colour40] %F\
#[fg=colour128] %T\
#[fg=colour202] %Z\
#[default]"

You can now easily have a Bash display useful information on the Tmux status line. I hope you learned something new and enjoyed reading this article.

Follow me on Dev.to and Github.

Feel free to leave comments, questions, and suggestions.

报告其他不当行为

原文:

Este artículo fue originalmente escrito en https://matiashernandez.dev

Typescript es un lenguaje completo (un lenguaje completo de Turing); por lo tanto, tiene algunas palabras clave con las que trabajar. Una de ellas es extends.

Esta palabra clave puede ser muy confusa ya que engloba un par de conceptos o significados que dependen del contexto en el que se utilice.

Herencia con extends

La herencia en Typescript se refiere a permitir crear nuevas interfaces que heredan o extienden el comportamiento de una anterior.

interface User { firstName: string; lastName: string; email: string;
} interface StaffUser extends User { roles: Array<Role>
}

Lo que se puede ver en el fragmento anterior es:

  • La interfaz User describe un tipo con algunas propiedades que representan a un usuario.
  • La interfaz StaffUser representa a un usuario con las mismas propiedades que User pero además una más: roles.
  • Este uso de extends se puede utilizar para heredar de múltiples interfaces simultáneamente con sólo utilizar nombres separados por comas de las interfaces base. 
interface StaffUser extends User, RoleUser { }

Este comportamiento también se puede utilizar para extender una Clase

extends como restricción.

También se puede utilizar extends para restringir el tipo o limitar el ámbito de un tipo.

export type QueryFunction< T = unknown, TQueryKey extends QueryKey = QueryKey,
> = (context: QueryFunctionContext<TQueryKey>) => T | Promise<T>

El ejemplo anterior (de una implementación real de tanstack-query) muestra un tipo llamado QueryFunction. Este es un tipo genérico que acepta dos valores: T y TQueryKey.

El uso genérico aquí es muy similar a una función en javascript, que acepta dos argumentos.

Lo que importa aquí es observar el uso de la palabra clave extends: TQueryKey extends QueryKey = QueryKey. Esto se puede leer como TQueryKey y debe ser de tipo Query con un valor por defecto de QueryKey.

El uso anterior de extends acotando o restringiendo el genérico es la piedra angular para poder implementar tipos condicionales ya que los extends se utilizarán como condición para comprobar si un genérico está fuera de un tipo determinado.


✉️ Únete a Micro-bytes 🐦 Sígueme en Twitter ❤️ Apoya mi trabajo

Muhammad Sifat Hossain - Nov 8

Items.Title: 代码第 11 天的到来 | Advent of Code Day 11
Items.Description:
• 介绍
  • 问题陈述
  • 代码
  • 一开始我花了太多时间试图巧妙地解析输入。 名为 captures 的 Regex 工作得很好，但我也尝试使用元编程动态创建函数。 最终我放弃了，对函数组合进行了硬编码。
  • 在为轮次、猴子和物品构建了三层整洁的 reduce 层之后，我意识到需要在轮次中修改状态，以便猴子可以向轮次中稍后出现的其他猴子扔东西。 这是一个典型的例子，其中功能范式实际上不太直观。 我在 Elixir 论坛上看到有人使用 GenServers，这是一种更程序化地思考状态的完美方式。 我选择将 round 和 monkey reduce 合并为一个 reduce，这样它们就可以共享同一个累加器。
  • 不可否认，我并没有自己弄清楚第二部分的诀窍。 我的大脑正朝着正确的方向前进，但我真的不想花时间解决数学问题。 一旦暂停，sethcalebweeks 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

解除暂停后，sethcalebweeks 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，sethcalebweeks 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 sethcalebweeks 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 sethcalebweeks：

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 sethcalebweeks 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

  • Intro
  • Problem Statement
  • Code
  • I spent way too much time at the beginning trying to be clever about parsing the input. The Regex named captures work great, but I also tried to dynamically create the functions using metaprogramming. Eventually I gave up on that and hard-coded the function combinations.
  • After constructing three neat layers of reduces for the rounds, monkeys, and items, I realized that the state needed to be modified within the round so that monkeys could throw to other monkeys coming later in the round. This is a prime example where a functional paradigm is actually less intuitive. I saw that one person on the Elixir forum used GenServers, which is a perfect way of thinking about state a little more procedurally. I opted for smushing the round and monkey reduces into a single reduce so that they could share the same accumulator.
  • Admittedly, I did not figure out the trick to the second part on my own. My brain was headed in the correct direction, but I didn't really want to spend my time figuring out a math problem. (Usually, I love doing math, but my primary goal has been to keep up with these Advent of Code problems.) 
defmodule Day11 do use AOC def new("old", "+", "old"), do: fn old -> old + old end def new(left, "+", "old"), do: fn old -> String.to_integer(left) + old end def new("old", "+", right), do: fn old -> old + String.to_integer(right) end def new("old", "*", "old"), do: fn old -> old * old end def new(left, "*", "old"), do: fn old -> String.to_integer(left) * old end def new("old", "*", right), do: fn old -> old * String.to_integer(right) end def parse_note(note) do info = Regex.named_captures(
~r/Monkey (?<monkey>\d): Starting items: (?<items>.*) Operation: new = (?<left>.*) (?<op>.*) (?<right>.*) Test: divisible by (?<test>.*) If true: throw to monkey (?<throw_true>.*) If false: throw to monkey (?<throw_false>.*)/, note) {String.to_integer(info["monkey"]), %{ count: 0, items: info["items"] |> String.split(", ") |> Enum.map(&String.to_integer/1), operation: new(info["left"], info["op"], info["right"]), test: fn test -> if rem(test, info["test"] |> String.to_integer) == 0 do info["throw_true"] |> String.to_integer() else info["throw_false"] |> String.to_integer() end end }} end def notes() do input(11) |> String.split("\n\n", trim: true) |> Map.new(&parse_note/1) end def part1 do notes = notes() Enum.reduce(1..(20 * map_size(notes)), notes, fn i, monkeys -> current_monkey = rem(i - 1, map_size(notes)) %{items: items, operation: operation, test: test} = monkeys[current_monkey] Enum.reduce(items, monkeys, fn item, state -> new_worry_level = operation.(item) |> div(3) state |> update_in([test.(new_worry_level), :items], fn items -> items ++ [new_worry_level] end) |> update_in([current_monkey, :items], fn [_ | items] -> items end) |> update_in([current_monkey, :count], fn count -> count + 1 end) end) end) |> Enum.sort_by(fn {_, %{count: count}} -> count end, :desc) |> Enum.take(2) |> Enum.reduce(fn {_, %{count: first_count}}, {_, %{count: second_count}} -> first_count * second_count end) end def part2 do notes = notes() Enum.reduce(1..(10_000 * map_size(notes)), notes, fn i, monkeys -> current_monkey = rem(i - 1, map_size(notes)) %{items: items, operation: operation, test: test} = monkeys[current_monkey] Enum.reduce(items, monkeys, fn item, state -> new_worry_level = operation.(item) |> rem(9699690) state |> update_in([test.(new_worry_level), :items], fn items -> items ++ [new_worry_level] end) |> update_in([current_monkey, :items], fn [_ | items] -> items end) |> update_in([current_monkey, :count], fn count -> count + 1 end) end) end) |> Enum.sort_by(fn {_, %{count: count}} -> count end, :desc) |> Enum.take(2) |> Enum.reduce(fn {_, %{count: first_count}}, {_, %{count: second_count}} -> first_count * second_count end) end end

Items.Title: Chat-gpt 正在扼杀工作！ | Chat-gpt is killing jobs!
Items.Description:
发表于像 GPT-3 这样的大型语言模型的兴起引发了很多关于它们对就业市场的潜在影响的讨论，其中一个受到特别关注的领域是编程领域。 一些人担心 GPT-3 和其他类似模型可能会取代人类程序员，导致该行业广泛失业。 这种担忧是可以理解的。 事实上，已经有人使用 GPT-3 生成了简单的程序，而且结果往往令人印象深刻。 给定正确的指令和数据，GPT-3 可以编写语法正确的代码，甚至可以解决简单的问题。 但是，需要注意的是，GPT-3 和其他大型语言模型尚无法取代人类程序员。 虽然他们可以编写语法正确的代码，但他们无法理解代码的底层逻辑和含义。 这意味着他们无法编写复杂的程序或调试现有代码。 此外，编程不仅仅是写代码。 它需要对所解决的问题有深刻的理解，具有逻辑和系统的思考能力，以及对复杂系统进行故障排除和调试的能力。 这些是难以自动化的技能，它们对于成功的编程至关重要。

因此，虽然 GPT-3 和其他大型语言模型可能能够生成简单的程序，但它们还不能取代人类程序员。 然而，随着这些模型不断发展并变得更加先进，它们有可能在未来实现编程的某些方面的自动化。 然而，就目前而言，人类程序员是安全的。

你读的整件事不可怕吗？ 那是 chat-gpt 😱 写的。
我刚刚输入"write a blog about chat gpt killing programming jobs ”，它给出了这个！。 这样比我的博客好多了。 如果你是作家，这东西可以代替你。 喜欢，试试吧，它是免费的。 让它写一个故事，它会给你一个以前没有人听过的原创故事。 这很可怕。

测试 1

让我们尝试创建一个“hello world”HTML 页面
很简单吧？ 再来一次！

测试 2

"write a javascript program that will find the biggest number in given three."
 回答 chat-gpt 给我的问题 =>

 "// define the three numbers var num1 = 5; var num2 = 10; var num3 = 15; // initialize the variable that will hold the largest number var largest = num1; // compare the first number with the second and third numbers // and update the value of the largest if necessary if (num2 > largest) { largest = num2; } if (num3 > largest) { largest = num3; } // print the largest number to the console console.log("The largest number is: " + largest);"

你知道javascript太容易了吗？ 让我们尝试组装！

测试 3

"Create an assembly program that will input three values and show the largest number."

这真是令人兴奋🤯。

 " ; This program inputs three values and shows the largest number section .data num1: dq 0 ; first number num2: dq 0 ; second number num3: dq 0 ; third number section .bss section .text global _start _start: ; Input first number mov rax, 0 ; syscall number for read mov rdi, 0 ; file descriptor (standard input) mov rsi, num1 ; address of buffer to store input mov rdx, 8 ; number of bytes to read syscall ; Input second number mov rax, 0 ; syscall number for read mov rdi, 0 ; file descriptor (standard input) mov rsi, num2 ; address of buffer to store input mov rdx, 8 ; number of bytes to read syscall ; Input third number mov rax, 0 ; syscall number for read mov rdi, 0 ; file descriptor (standard input) mov rsi, num3 ; address of buffer to store input mov rdx, 8 ; number of bytes to read syscall ; Compare the numbers and store the largest in rax mov rax, num1 cmp rax, num2 jg greater_than mov rax, num2 greater_than: cmp rax, num3 jg greater_than mov rax, num3 ; Output the largest number mov rax, 1 ; syscall number for write mov rdi, 1 ; file descriptor (standard output) mov rsi, rax ; address of buffer to output mov rdx, 8 ; number of bytes to write syscall ; Exit the program mov rax, 60 ; syscall number for exit mov rdi, 0 ; return value syscall "

越来越容易让我们最后一次尝试

测试 4

"create a binary program that will input a value, and will display it"

The rise of large language models like GPT-3 has sparked a lot of discussion about their potential impact on the job market, and one area that has come under particular scrutiny is the field of programming. Some people are concerned that GPT-3 and other similar models could potentially replace human programmers, leading to widespread job loss in the industry. The concern is understandable. GPT-3 is capable of generating human-like text, which means that it could potentially be used to write code. In fact, some people have already used GPT-3 to generate simple programs, and the results are often impressive. Given the right instructions and data, GPT-3 can write code that is syntactically correct and even solves simple problems. However, it's important to note that GPT-3 and other large language models are not yet capable of replacing human programmers. While they can write syntactically correct code, they are not capable of understanding the underlying logic and meaning of the code. This means that they are not able to write complex programs or debug existing code. In addition, programming is more than just writing code. It requires a deep understanding of the problem being solved, the ability to think logically and systematically, and the ability to troubleshoot and debug complex systems. These are skills that are difficult to automate, and they are essential for successful programming.

So, while GPT-3 and other large language models may be able to generate simple programs, they are not yet capable of replacing human programmers. However, as these models continue to evolve and become more advanced, it's possible that they could potentially automate some aspects of programming in the future. For now, however, human programmers are safe.

根据 TechSci 研究报告，“全球农业设备市场按类型（拖拉机、收割机、种植设备、灌溉和作物加工设备、喷洒设备、干草和饲料设备、其他）、按自动化（自动、半自动、手动），按销售渠道（OEM 与售后市场）、按应用（土地开发和苗床准备、播种和种植、杂草栽培、植物保护、收获和脱粒、收获后和农业加工、其他）、按地区、预测和机会，2025”，由于对提高农业部门运营效率的需求不断增长，预计全球农业设备市场在预测期内将以稳定的速度增长。

此外，政府提供大量补贴和以低利率向农民提供农业设备的支持性政策和计划预计将进一步推动到 2025 年的市场增长。此外，食品和饮料行业也有望蓬勃发展对预测期内的市场增长产生积极影响。

此外，技术进步和人工智能、物联网、GPS 和机器学习等在农业领域的出现有望进一步为市场增长创造有利可图的机会。 然而，农业设备的高成本可能会阻碍到 2025 年的市场增长。此外，农业设备市场高度分散，市场竞争激烈。

这反过来可能会对预测期内的市场增长产生负面影响。 此外，农民对不同农业设备缺乏认识，尤其是新兴世界的农民，预计将进一步限制未来几年的市场增长。 此外，熟练劳动力的缺乏预计也将在预测期内减缓市场增长

他谈到了他追求高等教育的雄心，通过了竞争性考试，被一所知名大学录取，但签证被拒绝了。

这对我来说听起来微不足道，因为作为一个母亲，我从他父母的眼中看着他，说这不是生命的尽头，亲爱的。 继续前行！！ 但对他来说，这是第一次失败，虽然暂时改变了他的职业生涯。

并回答了他的问题 - 有轻微的侮辱，沮丧和失落 - 多次内疚而死。 但每一个都让我变得更坚强，塑造了我更美好的明天。

没关系每个人都有不好的日子让我们暂停一下休息一会儿

重置和重启

请记住，没有信任，任何领导者都无法建立一支伟大的团队！ 让我们先构建它！

识别、开发和应用您的天赋和热情，以便您能够以对您最有意义的方式做出最好的贡献。

变革型领导者
有了这一切，你显然可以成为一个变革型领导者，并创造出这样有远见的领导者。

祝你在未来取得圆满成功！！

Embrace Failure Recently I met a very young and skilled professional and he asked me, mam, how would you face failure? Well, he is just out of college and less than a year into his IT job.

Items.Title: 使用 Supabase 存储进行客户端图像压缩 | Client-side image compression with Supabase Storage
Items.Description:
我一直从事的项目之一允许用户上传和共享图像。 超级小说，我知道。 我们使用Supabase作为后端，图像存储在 Supabase Storage——一个 S3 兼容的对象存储中。 我现在已经使用过几次 Supabase，发现它总体上是一个很好的产品（#notsponsored）。 然而，这是我第一次使用存储产品，我们从一开始就遇到了问题。 用户从他们的手机上传分辨率非常高的照片，因此文件非常大。 这不是很好，因为我们对存储和带宽有限制，而且应用程序的图库视图需要永远加载。

不幸的是，Supabase 目前不支持任何类型的图像优化。 它已被普遍要求，并且很可能在他们的路线图上，但至少目前还没有我可以快速切换以解决问题的设置。 在使用 Amazon S3 之前，我遇到过类似的问题，在那个项目中，我们使用 lambda 函数在将图像上传到存储桶时进行图像优化。 但这对于 Supabase 来说似乎不是特别简单，所以我开始寻找不同的解决方案。

在研究了几个不同的选项之后，我找到了一个由 Chen Fengyuan 编写的名为compressorjs的包。 这个库在浏览器中执行图像的压缩和大小调整。 起初我有点怀疑，但这是一个很酷的项目并且有很好的浏览器支持。 有一大堆转换选项。 对于我的项目，我发现只需将图像的最大宽度更改为 600px 并稍微降低质量即可大大减小文件大小。

以下是如何将 compressorjs 与 Supabase 一起使用：

 import Compressor from " compressorjs " ; document . getElementById ( " file " ). addEventListener ( " change " , ( e ) => { const image = e . target . files [ 0 ]; if ( ! image ) { return ; } new Compressor ( image , { quality : 0.9 , maxWidth : 600 , success ( compressedImage ) { // compressedImage contains the Blob // Upload the compressed image to Supabase storage supabase . storage . from ( " images " ) . upload ( `public/filename` , compressedImage ); }, error ( err ) { console . log ( err . message ); }, }); });

原文:

One of the projects I have been working on allows users to upload and share images. Super novel, I know. We are using Supabase for the backend, and the images are stored in Supabase Storage - an S3 compatible object store. I’ve used Supabase a few times now and found it overall to be a neat product (#notsponsored). However, this was my first time using the storage offering and we ran into an issue from the outset. Users were uploading photos from their phones with very large resolutions and consequently huge file sizes. This wasn’t good because we have limits on storage and bandwidth, plus the gallery view of the app was taking forever to load.

Unfortunately, Supabase does not currently support any kind of image optimization. It’s been commonly requested, and quite likely on their roadmap, but at least for now there wasn’t a setting I could quickly toggle to solve the problem. I’ve run into a similar issue before using Amazon S3, and in that project we used a lambda function to do image optimisation when an image was uploaded to the bucket. But this didn’t seem particularly straightforward with Supabase, so I went searching for a different solution.

After researching a couple of different options, what I landed on was a package called compressorjs written by Chen Fengyuan. This library performs compression and resizing of images in the browser. I was a bit skeptical at first, but it's a cool project and has good browser support. How Compressorjs works is that it uses the HTML5 canvas element to read the original image data and perform lossy transformations to compress and resize the image. There's a whole bunch of transformation options. For my project, what I have found is simply changing the maximum width of the image to 600px and slightly reducing the quality has greatly reduced the file sizes.

Here is how you might go about using compressorjs with Supabase:

import Compressor from "compressorjs"; document.getElementById("file").addEventListener("change", (e) => { const image = e.target.files[0]; if (!image) { return; } new Compressor(image, { quality: 0.9, maxWidth: 600, success(compressedImage) { // compressedImage contains the Blob // Upload the compressed image to Supabase storage supabase.storage .from("images") .upload(`public/filename`, compressedImage); }, error(err) { console.log(err.message); }, });
});

Items.Title: Gamify JS：用于轻松创建浏览器 roguelike 的资源 | Gamify JS: resources for easy browser roguelike creation
Items.Description:
科林威廉姆斯91

发表于也许你以前听说过 roguelike 视频游戏，或者你可能没有……但在这篇博客中，我将描述制作 roguelike 的基础以及如何在几个 JS 和 HTML 文件中完成它。 .也许还有一点你应该一路走来的原因。

什么是Roguelike ？

  • 角色扮演——每位玩家一次将以一位主要玩家的视角穿越游戏世界。
  • 通过这种方式，环境及其居民（有生命的和无生命的）通过每个随机组件的动态相互作用为玩家提供独特的体验。 更多内容如下：
  • 随机生成的项目- 不仅环境是随机的，而且填充它的所有东西也是随机的。 这可以很简单，例如有益的健康恢复物品或增加属性的设备的出现，也可以更复杂，例如可以与玩家结盟或干扰的非玩家角色！
  • 非模态能力——与许多当前的视频游戏不同，玩家可以根据他们在游戏中获得的任意数量的经验来解锁新技能或能力，roguelikes 开始的方式与他们在玩家头像的能力方面结束的方式相同。 玩家在开始游戏时就可以使用所有技能。 随着时间的推移，只有他们使用这些技能的能力和熟练程度才会提高。
  • 与之前提到的 roguelike 质量相关，每次玩家死亡时，他们将作为新角色（具有相同技能）在新世界中重新开始，唯一剩下的元神器是玩家自己对他们之前遇到的事情的记忆。

您可能会开始明白为什么roguelike游戏长期以来一直是计算机程序员的游乐场。 事实上，该类型的名称来自 1980 年的同名游戏Rogue ，这是一款基于文本的终端游戏。 但是，历史是另一篇文章......

如何打造 Roguelike

因此，作为初出茅庐的 Javascript 专家，我迫不及待地想在放学后亲自动手编写这些心爱游戏的代码。 对我来说幸运的是，在 JS 世界中有一个蓬勃发展的 roguelikes 社区。 下面是一个有用资源的目录，用于创建极简的 roguelikes，只有几百行 JS、CSS 和 HTML。

旋转.js

它的库托管工具可帮助开发人员： 

 console - rendering randomly - generated - numbers path - finding field - of - view map - generation keyboard - input tile - maps event - scheduling

使用以下命令调用访问 Rot.js 命名空间数据结构：

 ROT . Scheduler ROT . EventQueue ROT . Map ROT . Display ROT . Noise ROT . FOV ROT . Lighting ROT . Color ROT . Path ROT . Text ROT . StringGenerator

肯尼.nl

上面我简要提到了 tile-maps 作为 rot.js 的一个特性，而https://www.kenney.nl/assets/micro-roguelike是你可以获得 PNG 以使用 tile-mapping 从图像导入 1x1bit 精灵的地方 -资产为您的环境。

为了使用 rot.js 实现这个图块集，我们导入资产并将其分配给一个元素：
const tileSet = document.createElement("img");
tileSet.src = "kenney-micro-rogue.png";

然后我们将一些字符映射到我们选择的 1x1 精灵：

 var options = { layout: "tile", bg: "transparent", tileWidth: 40, tileHeight: 40, tileSet: tileSet, // micro-rogue asset tileMap: { // characters mapped "@": [4, 0], // --> ".": [4, 4], // --> "P": [11, 0], // --> "*": [9, 3], // --> }, width: 18, height: 20, } Object.keys(options.tileMap).forEach(function(k) { options.tileMap[k][0] *= options.tileWidth; options.tileMap[k][1] *= options.tileHeight; })

下面是我们映射的精灵！

剩下的代码用于创建地图并在地图渲染到 DOM 时初始化我们的游戏！

 this . display = new ROT . Display ( options ); var digger = new ROT . Map . Digger ( options . width , options . height ); tileSet . onload = function () { Game . init (); }

今天的最后一个资源是一个很棒的 8/16 位声音发生器应用程序，称为 SFXR。

它非常易于使用，您可以通过按“序列化”键轻松地将它们导出到您的编码环境中，这将输出一个 JSON 对象以供复制/粘贴。 但是，您也可以只在游戏代码中使用该库：

npm install jsfxr


<script src="https://sfxr.me/riffwave.js"></script>
<script src="https://sfxr.me/sfxr.js"></script>
正如我上面提到的，您可以使用他们的 webApp 生成声音对象或使用sfxr.generate(preset); 传递他们的pickupCoin, laserShoot, explosion, powerUp, hitHurt, jump, blipSelect, synth, tone, click, random presets 之一的方法。 使用这些方法中的任何一种来生成声音对象，然后您可以在代码中使用.play或使用.mutate进一步调制声音。

如果这些不足以开始使用 JS 和 HTML 创建 roguelike，那...

电子游戏有一种叫做“果汁”的品质。 游戏的“果汁”是指让游戏“感觉”良好的简单视觉增强。 这些可以是屏幕抖动、尘云、枪口闪光、次要对象物理、射弹追踪器等元素。这些调用都是使用 CSS 动画和@keyframes创建的！ 通过关键帧，开发人员可以操纵移动/位置、旋转、大小和不透明度等等！

 .grow-and-fade { animation : grow-and-fade 1s forwards ; } @keyframes grow-and-fade { from { transform : scale ( 1 ); opacity : 1 ; } to { transform : scale ( 3 ); opacity : 0 ; } }

我希望你们都觉得这本书读起来至少有点乐趣！ 入门非常容易，希望这可以成为您的起点！

并想知道我会在下面待多久。”

原文:

colinwilliams91

Posted on Dec 12

Maybe you've heard of a roguelike video game before, or maybe you haven't... but in this blog I will describe the foundations of what makes a roguelike and how to do it in a couple files of JS and HTML... and perhaps a little bit of why you should along the way.

What's a Roguelike?

  • Role-playing - Each player will traverse the game-world from the perspective of one main-player at a time.
  • Randomly Generated Dungeons - Roguelike games revolve around a central idea of randomization. Through this, the environment and its inhabitants (living and inanimate) provide unique experiences to the player through the dynamic interplay of each random component. More below:
  • Randomly Generated Items - Not only will the environment be random but everything that populates it will be as well. This can be simple such as the occurrence of beneficial health recovery items or stat-increasing equipment or more complex like non-player-characters that can align with the player or interfere!
  • Non-modal Abilities - Unlike a lot of current video games where players will unlock new skills or abilities based on an arbitrary amount of experience they gain in the game, roguelikes start the same way they end in regards to the capabilities of the player avatar. All skills are available to the player the moment they begin the game. Only their aptitude and proficiency of using those skills will improve over the time played.
  • Permadeath - A final pillar to the roguelike is that when you die... you're dead. Related to the previously mentioned quality of roguelikes each time a player dies they will begin again as a new character (with the same skills) in a new world and the only remaining meta-artifact is the player's own memory of what they have previously encountered.

You may begin to see why the roguelike has a long history of being computer programmers playgrounds. In fact, the genre's name comes from the titular game Rogue from 1980 which was a text-based terminal game. But, the history is for another post...

How to Build a Roguelike

So, the fledgling Javascript adept that I am couldn't wait till after school to try to get my hands dirty in the code involved in making these beloved games. Fortunately for me, there is a thriving community in the JS world for roguelikes. What follows is a cataloguing of useful resources for creating minimalist roguelikes with nothing more than a few hundred lines of JS, CSS, and HTML.

Rot.js

ROguelike Toolkit JavaScript (rot.js) is a JS library which facilitates the development of games upon the roguelike foundations I listed above. Rot.js is opensource, free, and the repo can be cloned here: https://github.com/ondras/rot.js#usage or you can use it like this:

npm install rot-js

Its library hosts tools which help the developer with:

console-rendering
randomly-generated-numbers
path-finding
field-of-view
map-generation
keyboard-input
tile-maps
event-scheduling

The Rot.js namespace data-structure is accessed is invoked with the following commands:

ROT.Scheduler
ROT.EventQueue
ROT.Map
ROT.Display
ROT.Noise
ROT.FOV
ROT.Lighting
ROT.Color
ROT.Path
ROT.Text
ROT.StringGenerator

Kenney.nl

Kenney is a free game assets library for developing minimalist games such as our roguelike project. Above I briefly mentioned tile-maps as a feature of rot.js and https://www.kenney.nl/assets/micro-roguelike is where you would get a PNG to use tile-mapping to import 1x1bit sprites from an image-asset for your environments.

To implement this tile set with rot.js we import the asset and assign it to an element:
const tileSet = document.createElement("img");
tileSet.src = "kenney-micro-rogue.png";

Then we map some characters to the 1x1 sprites of our choosing:

var options = {
    layout: "tile",
    bg: "transparent",
    tileWidth: 40,
    tileHeight: 40,
    tileSet: tileSet, // micro-rogue asset
    tileMap: {        // characters mapped
        "@": [4, 0],  // -->
        ".": [4, 4],  // -->
        "P": [11, 0], // -->
        "*": [9, 3],  // -->
    },
    width: 18,
    height: 20,
}

Object.keys(options.tileMap).forEach(function(k) {
  options.tileMap[k][0] *= options.tileWidth;
  options.tileMap[k][1] *= options.tileHeight;
})

*code from Chris McCormick roguelikle-celebration-2021 tutorial repo annotations by me. Below are the sprites we mapped!

And the rest of the code to create the map and initialize our Game when the map has rendered to the DOM!

this.display = new ROT.Display(options); var digger = new ROT.Map.Digger(options.width, options.height); tileSet.onload = function() { Game.init();
}

SFXR.me

And the last resource for today is an awesome 8/16 bit sound generator App called SFXR.

It's super easy to use and you can easily export them into your coding environment by pressing the "serialize" key which will output a JSON object for copy/paste. But, you can also just use the library inside your game code:

npm install jsfxr


<script src="https://sfxr.me/riffwave.js"></script>
<script src="https://sfxr.me/sfxr.js"></script>
As I mentioned above, you can use their webApp to generate sound objects OR use the sfxr.generate(preset); method passing in one of their pickupCoin, laserShoot, explosion, powerUp, hitHurt, jump, blipSelect, synth, tone, click, random presets. Using either of these ways to generate a sound object you can then utilize it in your code with .play or further modulate the sound with .mutate.

In case those aren't enough reasons to start creating a roguelike with JS, and HTML, what about...

CSS

Video games have a quality called "juice". The "juice" of a game refers to simple visual enhancements that make the game "feel" good. These can be elements such as screenshake, dust clouds, muzzleflash, minor object-physics, projectile tracers, etc. And these call all be created using CSS animations and @keyframes! Through keyframes a developer can manipulate movement/position, rotation, size, and opacity to list a few!

.grow-and-fade { animation: grow-and-fade 1s forwards;
}
@keyframes grow-and-fade { from { transform: scale(1); opacity: 1; } to { transform: scale(3); opacity: 0; }
}

I hope you all found this a least mildly fun to read! It's super easy to get started and hopefully this can be your jump-off point!

"As the moon burned bright above, I paid my Bedouin guide,

And wondered how long I'd be below."

(randomly generated intro narration from one of my favorite roguelikes: Spelunky)

WORKS CITED

https://github.com/chr15m/roguelike-celebration-2021 - Chris McCormick tutorial repo

Items.Description:
布雷迪·多尔西

发表于各位开发者大家好，今天我将写下我学习 ReactJS 的经历，并向您展示我刚刚创建的聊天应用程序中的一些示例。

对于那些不熟悉 React 的人来说，它是一个使用组件和 props 以更有条理的方式呈现代码的编程框架。

下面是我的 index.js 文件的片段。

您看到的是我的 React 应用程序的顶层。 在深入了解细节之前，最好先了解一下我的项目文件夹是什么样子的。

在我的项目目录的 src 文件夹中，我有我的渲染文件 (index.js)、我的主要应用程序组件 (App.js) 和我的样式表 (App.css)。

您还可以看到一个组件文件夹，其中包含我在此项目中使用的每个组件的下拉列表。 带有他们尊敬的头像个人资料图像的用户名。 这里的关键是要注意 NavBar.js、Home.js、Chat.js、Users.js 和 Login.js 是我的主要 App 组件的直接子组件，将在我的路由处理中使用。

现在让我们谈谈我的 App 组件的实现。

首先，我包括我的每一个组件，

应用程序.js

然后我在组件的开始部分声明了一些状态。 如果您不知道“状态”是什么，它是一个内置于框架中的反应对象，用于保存数据。 每当更新此状态的数据时，组件都会重新呈现。

应用程序.js

我在这里声明的状态负责检查用户是否登录，包含所有用户数据，以及包含您登录的任何用户的数据。

现在是我的退货声明：

应用程序.js

在最顶部，您可以看到首先呈现的是我的 NavBar 组件，我将很快在这里简要介绍它。 然后您会看到我的每条路线都包裹在一个 Switch 中。 开关在那里查看每个子路由并显示与当前 URL 匹配的第一个。 当您尝试处理具有多个页面的多个路由时，这非常有用。 这些路线将确保我能够一次看到这些组件之一。 正如您所看到的，我在这些组件中有一堆道具，例如在聊天和用户组件中，我们有 isLoggedIn 道具，这些道具仅由登录中的道具 (setIsLoggedIn) 触发。

现在，在所有这些之后，让我向您展示我的家庭组件以及我必须实际展示的内容。

首页.js

我的 NavBar 按预期呈现在最顶部，而 Home 是选定的路线，因此目前正在呈现

接下来是聊天组件

聊天.js

我制作了 2 个 useState 挂钩，消息保存所有消息/评论数据，而 formData 跟踪评论表单框中的内容。 我还使用了我用来从服务器检索消息数据的 useEffect 挂钩。

聊天.js

下一个函数是我的删除函数，它由删除按钮上的 onClick 侦听器调用，它向服务器发送 DELETE 请求，过滤与单击的对象 ID 匹配的任何项目。

聊天.js

你在这里看到的是我的这个组件的事件监听器。 每当你想添加评论时，handleSubmit 将读取表单数据中的内容，检查它是否为空，然后继续向服务器发送 POST 请求，并更新消息状态。

最后我们到了聊天组件的返回语句，

聊天.js

为此，我实际上使用引导框架来帮助我使用聊天框 AI，messagesToDisplay 包装在 Card 和 ListGroup 中，每当调用 messagesToDisplay 时，它都会遍历消息状态并将其值分配给 Messages将实际消息呈现到页面的组件。

消息.js

我还想指出聊天组件中的这一行：
这是在呈现页面之前检查用户是否登录的条件。 如果为 false，则用户将被重定向回登录页面 Chat 页面

让我们检查一下 Users 组件

用户.js

我声明我的用户状态保存我将用于为页面上的用户卡分配值的数据。 我使用 useEffect 挂钩来设置用户状态，检查用户是否在呈现之前登录，然后非常类似于我附加消息组件的方式，遍历每个用户并分配一个键、用户名和头像 URL 来显示每个用户头像和用户名。

用户.js

现在，这就是乐趣开始的地方……登录组件

登录.js

我声明我的 formData 状态并包含必要的组件。

登录.js

这一堆意大利面条是我对用户验证的解决方案。 提交表单后，它被传递到 checkLoginData 函数，它开始遍历每个用户以查看提交的用户是否存在，然后通过密码检查以查看它是否与后端的密码匹配。 如果用户存在，但密码错误，则会警告用户密码无效并返回，但不会发生任何事情。 但是，如果用户名在服务器中不存在，则会发出 POST 请求以创建用户，以便他们可以使用刚刚提交的凭据登录。

登录.js

以上是对用于提供验证的服务器的 GET 请求，以及对新用户的 POST 请求。

登录.js

这些是事件监听器，它们的工作方式与其他组件中的事件监听器非常相似

登录.js
最后我们有我们的 return 语句，它是将要在页面上呈现的内容。 每个输入都包含 formData 的值和触发表单更改和提交的事件侦听器

最后但同样重要的是，NavBar 组件

导航栏.js


这里的特别之处在于我包含的 NavLink。 它就像但它有一个属性可以判断什么是“活动的”并根据它改变样式。 这就是为什么我直接在这个组件中编写我的样式，这样我就可以对其进行一些控制，并通过更改活动按钮的颜色向用户显示他所在的页面。

这就是我的整个项目，还有很多重构需要完成，但我发现这是一个非常好的项目，可以让我涉足 React，我个人觉得上手非常有挑战性，但一旦你知道如何去做，就会感到非常满足让事情正常进行。

Brady Dorsey

Posted on Dec 12

Hello my fellow devs, today I am going to write about my experience learning ReactJS and show you some examples from a chat application I have just created.

For those who are not familiar to React, it is a programming framework that uses components and props in order to render your code in a much more organized manner.

Below is a snippet of my index.js file.

What you see is the top level of my react application. The DOM finds the root element of the project and our component is being rendered inside of it, nested by which essentially is what's responsible for the apps router implementation and making sure the UI is in sync with the URL.

Before I get into the nitty gritty, it may be best to have an idea of what my project folders look like.

Within the src folder of my project directory I have my render file (index.js), my main app component (App.js), and my stylesheet (App.css).

You can also see a components folder with a dropdown of each one of my components that I am using in this project. Chat.js is the chatroom component, Login.js is the login page component, Users.js is for displaying the servers existing profiles; the username with their respected avatar profile image. Home.js is self explanatory, it's the homepage! And NavBar.js is of course, the navigation bar which we use to switch between our routes.

It is key here to note that NavBar.js, Home.js, Chat.js, Users.js, and Login.js are the direct children to my main App component and will be used in my route handling.

Now let's talk about the implementation for my App component.

First I include each one of my components,

App.js

Then I declare a few states at the beginning part of the component. If you are unaware what a 'state' is, it is a react object built in to the framework which is used to hold data. Whenever the data to this state is updated, the component re-renders.

App.js

The states I have declared here are responsible for checking to see if user is logged in, containing all users data, and containing the data of whatever user you are logged into.

Now for my return statement:

App.js

At the very top you can see the first thing rendered is my NavBar component, which I will talk about briefly here soon. You then see each one of my routes wrapped in a Switch. The switch is there to look through each child route and display the first one that matches the current URL. This is great when you are trying to deal with multiple routes with multiple pages. These routes will make sure I'm able to see one of these components at a time. And as you can see I have a bunch of props in these components, such as in the Chat and Users components we have isLoggedIn props that are only triggered by a prop (setIsLoggedIn) within Login.

NOW then, after all of that let me show you my home component and what I have to actually show.

Home.js

I have my NavBar rendered at the very top as expected, and Home is the selected route so it is currently being rendered

Next is the chat component

Chat.js

I have made 2 useState hooks, messages holds all the message/comment data, and formData keeps track of what's in the comment form box. I also used the useEffect hook I am using to retrieve the messages data from the server.

Chat.js

The next function down is my delete function which is invoked by an onClick listener on the delete button, it sends a DELETE request to the server, filtering any item that matches the clicked objects id.

The getChatData() function is what you see invoked in the useEffect hook, which automatically fetch the messages data from the backend and return them to be appended into the chatbox

Chat.js

What you see here is my event listeners for this component. handleChange updates formDatas state every single change that is made before submitted. Whenver you want to add a comment, handleSubmit will read what's in the form data, check to see if its empty or not, then proceed to send POST request to the server, and update the messages state.

Finally we get to the Chat components return statement,

Chat.js

For this I actually used the bootstrap framework to help me a bit with the chatbox AI, messagesToDisplay is wrapped in a Card and a ListGroup, whenever messagesToDisplay is called it is going to iterate through the messages state and assign the values from it into a Messages component which is what renders the actual messages to the page.

Messages.js

I would also like to point out this one line in the Chat component:
This is the condition that checks if the user is logged in before even rendering the page. If false, then the user is redirected back to the login page Chat page

Let's check out the Users component

Users.js

I declare my users state to hold the data I will be using to assign the values for the User cards on the page. I used a useEffect hook to set the users state, check if users logged in before rendering, then very similar to how I appended my Messages components, iterating through each user and assigning a key, the username, and the avatarURL to display each users avatar and username.

User.js

Now, this is where the fun begins.. the Login component

Login.js

I declare my formData state and include neccesary components.

Login.js

This bunch of spaghetti was my solution to user validation. Once form is submitted it is passed into the checkLoginData function, it begins iterating through each user to see if the submitted user exists, it then goes through a password check to see if it matches the password in the backend. If the user exists, but password is wrong, the user is alerted of invalid password and returned with nothing happening. However if the username does not exist in the server, a POST request will be made to create a user so that they may login with the credentials that was just submitted.

Login.js

Above is the GET request to the server that is being used to provide validation, and the POST request for new users.

Login.js

These are the event listeners, that work very similar to the event listeners in the other components

Login.js
Finally we have our return statement which is what is going to be rendered on the page. Each input contains the value for formData and the event listeners to trigger the forms change and submission

Last but not least, the NavBar component

NavBar.js


What's special here is the NavLink I've included. It's just like but it has a property that can tell what is 'active' and change styles based off that. This is why I wrote my styles directly inside this component so I can have some control of that and show the user which page he is on by changing the color of the active button.

That is just about my whole project, there is still much refactoring to be done but I found this to be a really good project to dip my feet into React, which I personally found very challenging to pick up but very satisfying once you know how to get things working.

Here is the link to my repositories front end:

https://github.com/fusion407/chat-app-frontend

back end:

https://github.com/fusion407/chat-app-backend

原文:

Eduardo Bueno

Posted on Dec 12

Oi, eu sou o Edu!

Não sou um especialista em Kubernetes (ou K8s, como a comunidade gosta de chamá-lo). Nas palavras do meu amigo Ricardo Tenório, quando se trata de mexer com este orquestrador de contêineres eu "sou apenas um menino".

Mas, para resolver um problema (obter, a partir de testes de desempenho/carga, indicadores importantes sobre a capacidade de carga e transações que uma aplicação pode suportar), veio a oportunidade de entender mais a fundo esta ferramenta cuja finalidade é "facilitar a implantação e o gerenciamento de sistemas distribuídos, enquanto se beneficia do melhor uso de recursos de memória e processamento que os contêineres possibilitam", então resolvi aproveitar esta chance para, quem sabe, passar do nível Aprendiz para o nível Praticante.

Daí comecei a pesquisar sobre o assunto e acabei "caindo" no excelente vídeo do Jeferson Fernando da LINUXTips:

Esse vídeo me ajudou muito, pois apresentou de forma bastante clara os conceitos-chave do K8s, e também seus principais comandos. E isso me deu confiança para configurar o ambiente que eu pudesse utilizar para executar os testes que precisava!

Em relação aos testes, eu já havia utilizado o Postman e o RESTfull Stress para executar este tipo de teste, mas o cenário naquela ocasião era um tanto diferente: lá eu não estava testando uma aplicação hospedada dentro de um cluster no K8s! E, embora o princípio do teste seja o mesmo para este caso, o fato de haver mais camadas entre o cliente que faz a requisição e a aplicação a ser testada pode mascarar seus indicadores - vamos lembrar que, em linhas gerais, uma aplicação hospedada no K8s está rodando dentro de um Container que, por sua vez, encontra-se dentro de um Pod no Cluster.

Então fui pesquisar formas de executar estes testes de dentro do Cluster, e acabei encontrando uma alternativa que envolve o uso do K6 (ferramenta que eu também já conhecia e havia utilizado, e que gosto muito porque entre outras coisas ela permite a criação de scripts de teste em javascript, além da execução dos testes tanto de forma manual - na máquina do desenvolvedor - quanto automaticamente, como um passo dentro de uma pipeline de CI/CD, por exemplo).

No próximo post vou descrever em detalhes tudo o que fiz para resolver meu problema utilizando o K6, porém deixo aqui um gostinho do resultado final da minha PoC pra vocês ficarem curiosos. 😂

Abaixo, o resultado de um teste de carga em uma aplicação, executando o K6 localmente a partir da CLI do VS Code:

E o resultado do mesmo teste, só que executado dentro do Cluster do K8s:

Items.Title: 切片 | Slicing
Items.Description:
贾斯汀·萨伯鲁

发表于第一次开发网站时，开发人员会坐在一起思考应用程序的想法/目的，然后坐下来讨论数据库架构、前端样式、后端功能等。本质上将软件的每个大“块”拆分为设计和详细说明它需要和处理的目的和最小可行过程。

水平切片

以上将典型的软件开发规划称为水平切片。 这不是一个坏方法，而且对于开发团队之间的开发工作来说似乎相当实用。 唯一的问题是它没有考虑用户的体验和与产品的交互，或者它被称为用户故事。 在敏捷开发中，尤其是在最终产品面向数百万用户的情况下，明智的做法是优先考虑并将开发视角转向用户体验。

将您的代码拆分为功能层，例如蛋糕的呈现方式，这对于清晰地组织某些编程的内容和方式非常有用，但最终您可以想象水平切片的蛋糕不会让用户均匀地咬一口整个蛋糕产品。

更实际地说，水平切片出现的问题包括

  • 在项目完全完成之前，利益相关者无法看到项目的进展/部分

  • 如果一个小功能不匹配，利益相关者的反馈可能会完全改变产品（因为整个应用程序已经是最小可行产品 (MVP)

  • 较小的用户故事的评估被延迟到所有部分都完成，这阻碍了数据科学家评估成功的能力

垂直切片

垂直切片的主要方面是在用户故事的“切片”中思考。 例如，首先开发一个登录/注册功能，将加密数据存储在数据库中，并在需要时对其进行哈希处理，同时将前端路由以处理未经授权的用户到注册页面，并在获得授权后将其重定向。 这种方法允许您处理应用程序的所有方面，并且只开发初始垂直切片所需的内容。 后端的开发足以处理新的和现有的用户调用，前端可以处理从主页和注册页面路由和重定向，反之亦然。

这也为利益相关者提供了更好的展示，只展示了从用户的角度开发的最小部分，这样当要求进行不可避免的轻率和荒谬的更改时，它是可以容忍的，因为应用程序需要“返工”的部分更少

其他好处包括

  • 数据科学家能够更早地进行评估
  • 开发团队更符合利益相关者的理想和需求，而不会完全放弃他们自己对产品在幕后工作方式的理想。

结论

这两种发展流程在各自方面都很棒。 如果您正在为自己制作一个简单的应用程序，我什至会建议水平切片。 但是对于更大的规模，尤其是在团队中，垂直切片似乎涵盖了管理错误的所有理由，同时仍在推进您的代码

数据科学过程联盟https://www.datascience-pm.com/vertical-vs-horizo​​ntal- slicing-data-science-deliverables/#:~:text=Traditional%20software%20approaches%20favor%20developing,value%20streams% 20（垂直%20切片）。

视觉范式https://www.visual-paradigm.com/scrum/user-story-splitting-vertical-slice-vs-horizo ​​ntal-slice/

星期一博客https://monday.com/blog/project-management/vertical-slice/

原文:

Justin Saborouh

Posted on Dec 12

When first developing a website, developers would sit together, think of the idea/purpose of the application, then sit through to discuss database schema, front end styling, back end functionality, etc. Essentially splitting up each big "chunk" of software to design and detail the purposes and minimum viable processes it would require and handle.

The Horizontal Slice

The above references typical software development planning as horizontal slicing. This isn't a bad approach, and seems fairly practical for development work split amongst a dev team. The only concern is that it does not consider the user's experience and interaction with the product, or as it's called the User Stories. In agile development, especially where the end product is meant for millions of users, it is wise to prioritize and pivot the perspective of development towards the User Experience.

Splitting your code up into functional layers, like how the cake presents, is great to cleanly organize what and how certain programming is done, but in the end you can imagine that a horizontally sliced cake does not give a user an even bite of the entire product.

In more practical terms, issues that arise with horizontal slicing include

  • A stakeholder not being able to see progress/parts of the project until it is completely finished

  • Feedback from the stakeholder can change the product entirely if one small feature doesn't match (since the entire app was already an Minimum Viable Product (MVP)

  • Evaluation of smaller user stories is delayed until all parts are completed, which hinders data scientists' ability to evaluate success

The Vertical Slice

The main aspect of vertical slicing is thinking in "slices" of user stories. For example, first develop a login/signup feature that stores the encrypted data in the database and can hash it when needed, while simultaneously routing the frontend to handle unauthorized users to a signup page and redirecting them once authorized. This approach allows you to work on all aspects of the app and only develop what is needed for that initial vertical slice. The backend is developed enough to handle new and existing user calling, and the frontend can handle routing and redirecting from a home page and signup page, vice versa.

This also yields a better showcase to the stakeholder, only showing the minimal pieces developed in perspective of the user so that when the inevitable rash and absurd changes are asked for, it is slightly tolerable since there is less of the app to "rework"

Other benefits include

  • Data scientists being able to evaluate a lot earlier
  • The dev team being more aligned with the stakeholder's ideals and needs without completely forfeiting their own ideals of how the product works behind the scenes.

Conclusion

Both flows of development are great in their own regards. I would even suggest horizontal slicing if you are making a simple app for yourself. But for larger scale, and especially in teams, vertical slicing seems to cover all grounds of management error while still progressing your code

Resources

Data Science Process Alliance https://www.datascience-pm.com/vertical-vs-horizontal-slicing-data-science-deliverables/#:~:text=Traditional%20software%20approaches%20favor%20developing,value%20streams%20(vertical%20slicing).

Visual Paradigm https://www.visual-paradigm.com/scrum/user-story-splitting-vertical-slice-vs-horizontal-slice/

mondayBlog https://monday.com/blog/project-management/vertical-slice/
